description: Server metadata, character identity, ability scores, and scalar normalization fixtures.

origin.source: Constructed from PRD source protocol facts for confirmed server/client MSDP variables.

fixtures.description: Confirmed metadata variables parse as scalar pairs with numeric metadata normalized.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms SERVER_ID, SERVER_TIME, and SNIPPET_VERSION.
server/index.ts normalizes integer scalar strings to numbers before mapping.
notes: SERVER_TIME and SNIPPET_VERSION demonstrate parser-level integer normalization.

origin.source: Constructed from PRD source protocol facts for confirmed character MSDP variables.

fixtures.description: Character identity fields parse as scalars while level normalizes to a number.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms CHARACTER_NAME, LEVEL, RACE, CLASS, and ALIGNMENT.
notes: The character name is synthetic and not a live capture.
Level is a numeric string and should parse to number 17.

origin.source: Constructed from PRD source protocol facts for confirmed ability score MSDP variables.

fixtures.description: Ability score variables parse as normalized numeric scalars.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms STR, INT, WIS, DEX, CON, and CHA.
notes: Permanent ability variants are confirmed by the source facts but left for later coverage if the client requests them.

origin.source: Constructed from current parser normalization behavior in server/index.ts.

fixtures.description: Integer strings with leading zeros, zero, and negative signs normalize to numbers; non-integers remain strings.
sourceFacts: server/index.ts normalizeScalar uses the /^-?\d+$/ integer pattern.
PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms LEVEL, ATTACK_BONUS, AC, and POSITION.
notes: This is a parser-level fixture, not a claim that every numeric example is a common live value.
